title: HeliosAuth Service Overview
text:
Title: HeliosAuth Service Overview

The HeliosAuth service is maintained by the Infra team at NovaTech Systems. It handles a core production workflow and is documented in the internal service catalog.

- Default Port: 7418
- Authentication Key: CMD-5594
- Token Expiry: 15 minutes
- Primary Region: eu-west-2

The service depends on HarborWorker and AtlasWorker for upstream validation and request routing. Engineers should use the documented port and key values when configuring internal integrations.

title: EmberProxy API Reference
text:
Title: EmberProxy API Reference

The EmberProxy API is owned by the Monitoring team and is used for internal service-to-service requests.

- Endpoint: /emberproxy/v1/fetch
- Method: DELETE
- Rate Limit: 120 requests per minute
- API Key Prefix: RE-9973
- Timeout: 8 seconds

All requests must include the X-Service-Key header. Requests exceeding the documented rate limit may be throttled by the edge gateway.

title: VectorSync Deployment Configuration
text:
Title: VectorSync Deployment Configuration

The VectorSync deployment profile is maintained by the Messaging team and is used by release engineers during production rollouts.

- Default Replica Count: 4
- Cluster Region: us-central-1
- Auto-scaling Threshold: 75% CPU
- Rollback Window: 15 minutes
- Cluster Name: nt-prod-cluster-2

If the auto-scaling threshold is exceeded, the system expands replica capacity automatically. Failed deployments are eligible for rollback within the rollback window.

title: ZephyrGateway Monitoring Profile
text:
Title: ZephyrGateway Monitoring Profile

The ZephyrGateway monitoring profile is used by the Compliance team to track production health and trigger alerts.

- Alert Threshold: 75% CPU
- Check Interval: 60 seconds
- Incident ID Format: FZW-XXXX
- Consecutive Check Requirement: 2

Alerts are triggered when the threshold is exceeded for the documented number of consecutive checks. Monitoring events are visible in the central NovaTech incident dashboard.

title: Data Retention Policy
text:
Title: Data Retention Policy

The Data Retention Policy applies to the Engineering department and is enforced through internal compliance workflows.

- Log Retention: 90 days
- Backup Frequency: every 6 hours
- Approval Threshold: $3000
- Archive Storage: VaultStore

Records that exceed the approval threshold require manager review. Archived materials are stored in the documented archive system according to policy.

text:

title: SummitRouter Incident Runbook
text:
Title: SummitRouter Incident Runbook

This runbook is maintained by the Monitoring team and should be used when the system experiences a SEV-1 incident.

- Trigger Threshold: 85% CPU
- Primary Response Action: fail over to the backup region
- Fallback Region: eu-west-2
- Runbook Code: RBK-7738

If the trigger threshold is sustained during a high-severity event, on-call engineers should execute the primary action first. If recovery fails, traffic should be shifted to the fallback region.

title: VectorAuth Incident Runbook
text:
Title: VectorAuth Incident Runbook

This runbook is maintained by the Platform team and should be used when the system experiences a SEV-1 incident.

- Trigger Threshold: 70% CPU
- Primary Response Action: invalidate active tokens
- Fallback Region: ap-south-1
- Runbook Code: RBK-9513

If the trigger threshold is sustained during a high-severity event, on-call engineers should execute the primary action first. If recovery fails, traffic should be shifted to the fallback region.

text:
Title: VectorSync Incident Runbook

- Fallback Region: us-central-1

If the trigger threshold is sustained during a high-severity event, on-call engineers should execute the primary action first. If recovery fails, traffic should be shifted to the fallback region.

title: SignalCache Incident Runbook
text:
Title: SignalCache Incident Runbook

- Trigger Threshold: 85% CPU
- Primary Response Action: scale replicas to 5
- Fallback Region: eu-west-2

If the trigger threshold is sustained during a high-severity event, on-call engineers should execute the primary action first. If recovery fails, traffic should be shifted to the fallback region.

title: VectorSync Incident Runbook
text:
